name: Central Bank of Denver, N.A. v. First Interstate Bank of Denver, N.A.
summary: Held that there is no implied private right of action for aiding and abetting violations under Section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act.
significance: Cited by Petitioners and Justice Gorsuch as an example of the Court refusing to infer secondary liability from a statute that does not explicitly provide for it, counseling caution in expanding implied causes of action.
